front: Play: Helene Weigel performed a famous 'silent scream' accompanied by increasingly loud bass and treble pitch after refusing to acknowledge the body of her son Swiss Cheese
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: the title character's children are named Kattrin, Eilif, and Swiss Cheese; they are all lost during the Thirty Years' War
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: the prostitute Yvette sings the 'Fraternization Song,' set on a 'dark May night' with 'boys with mischief in their eyes'
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: ends with the protagonist singing a lullaby and hitching herself to a wagon
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: Rose Mbowa adapted it as Mother Uganda and Her Children; first authorized African-language production starred Mbowa
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: Margarete Steffin was a collaborator on this work set during the Thirty Years' War
back: Mother Courage and Her Children (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: the singer of 'Pirate Jenny' is bribed by Polly Peachum to turn Polly's husband over
back: The Threepenny Opera (Bertolt Brecht and Kurt Weill)

front: Play: Elisabeth Hauptmann is sometimes purported to have written the lion's share of this work co-created with Kurt Weill
Play: the author fought bitterly with director G.W. Pabst over the 1931 film adaptation starring Rudolf Forster and Lotte Lenya
Play: Wole Soyinka adapted this work as Opera Wonyosi
Play: a Singer performs works about 'The Four Generals' and 'The Rotten Bridge'
Play: an actor laments 'There must, there must, be some end that would fit'; ends with 'There must be happy endings, must, must, must!'
Play: a cup with two bottoms exposes one character as a swindler
Play: Athol Fugard named his Circle Players after this work
Play: the protagonist puts on a mask to impersonate her male cousin Shui Ta and sings 'without trampling down twelve others / You cannot help one poor man'
back: The Good Person of Szechwan (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: three gods give the protagonist funds to buy a tobacco shop; at the end they float away on a cloud, ignoring cries for help
back: The Good Person of Szechwan (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: adapted by Mohammed ben Abdallah as Land of a Million Magicians; translated into Swahili by John Ruganda
back: The Good Person of Szechwan (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: the protagonist frames Fish for a warehouse fire while working with the Cauliflower Trust; Chicago gangsters expand into Cicero, Illinois
back: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui (Bertolt Brecht)
work: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui

front: Play: Arturo Ui seduces Ignatius Dullfeet's wife Betty in a scene paralleling Richard III
back: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui (Bertolt Brecht)
work: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui

front: Play: epilogue warns 'don't rejoice too soon at your escape- / The womb he crawled from still is going strong'
back: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui (Bertolt Brecht)
work: The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui

front: Play: the title character is a lieutenant in the Black Straw Hats who organizes meat packers against tycoon Pierpont Mauler
back: Saint Joan of the Stockyards (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: a song declares 'you folk that are poor / are an army vast and grand' sung to workers outside the Lennox packing plant
back: Saint Joan of the Stockyards (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: revised with Charles Laughton after the atomic bombing of Japan; ends with the Discorsi being smuggled out of Florence by Andrea Sarti
Play: the title character's daughter Virginia dies of dysentery in a convent after being unable to find a husband
Playwright: wrote 'A Short Organum for the Theatre' to describe epic theatre and the gestus technique
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Playwright: essay 'On Chinese Acting' discusses how 'the actor looks at himself' and 'confines himself to merely quoting the character' as in Mei Lanfang's performances
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Playwright: Augusto Boal developed the Theater of the Oppressed as an alternative to this author's didactic epic theater
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Playwright: Frederic Jameson claimed this author stripped back the 'well-made production' by forcing actors to verbalize alternative choices
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Concept: Walter Benjamin wrote that this theatrical genre 'eliminated the Aristotelian catharsis' through 'untragic heroes'
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Concept: characters break down the 'Not/But' element by embodying social commentary with the gestus technique in this theatrical genre
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Playwright: the minimalist, artificial staging of Dogville was influenced by this author
work: Epic Theatre and the Alienation Effect

front: Poet: 'Alabama Song' begins 'show me the way / to the next whiskey bar' and was later recorded by The Doors
Poet: 'Reminiscence of Marie A.' describes a kiss under a plum tree remembered only because a white cloud passed overhead
Poet: 'Children's Hymn' was written as a response to Germany's national anthem
Poet: 'How Fortunate the Man with None' observes that 'sagacious Solomon,' 'courageous Caesar,' and 'honest Socrates' all met ignominious fates
Play: set in 1912 Chicago, Shlink and Garga engage in a metaphorical boxing match
back: In the Jungle of Cities (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: caused a riot when the clown Smith is dismembered by friends to ease his pain; an example of 'learning-plays'
back: The Baden-Baden Lesson on Consent (Bertolt Brecht)

front: Play: four Communist agents explain their decision to murder a comrade during a Chinese revolution
Novel: the one-legged George Fewkoombey murders William Coax with his prosthetic leg; falsely convicted for drowning Mary Sawyer
Playwright: suggested the idea for The Cradle Will Rock by Marc Blitzstein and was its dedicatee
Playwright: only Hollywood screenplay was Hangmen Also Die, directed by a fellow German expat
Playwright: wrote slapstick film Mysteries of a Barbershop starring Karl Valentin, a major influence
Play: set in Finland, the chauffeur Matti holds up a landowner's plan to marry his daughter Eva to a diplomat; based on The Sawdust Princess
back: Mr Puntila and His Man Matti (Bertolt Brecht)
